title: Cisco EtherChannels
description: Learn how to bundle physical links into EtherChannels using LACP, configure Port-channels, and verify channel status for redundancy and increased bandwidth.

tasks.explanation: EtherChannel configuration requires Privileged EXEC mode on the switch.

tasks.hint: Use the command: 'configure terminal' or 'conf t'
tasks.explanation: EtherChannel settings are applied from global configuration mode.

tasks.prompt: Enter Interface Configuration mode for GigabitEthernet0/1 and GigabitEthernet0/2 using a range.
tasks.explanation: 'interface range' applies identical configuration to multiple physical ports that will form one logical bundle.

tasks.prompt: Assign these interfaces to EtherChannel group 1 using LACP active mode.
tasks.explanation: LACP active mode initiates negotiation with the neighbor. The group number must match on both switches.

tasks.prompt: Enter Interface Configuration mode for GigabitEthernet0/3 and GigabitEthernet0/4 using a range.
tasks.explanation: Additional physical links can join the same EtherChannel group to increase aggregate bandwidth.

tasks.prompt: Assign GigabitEthernet0/3 and 0/4 to EtherChannel group 1 using LACP passive mode.
tasks.explanation: LACP passive mode responds to negotiation requests but does not initiate them. Active and passive modes are compatible.

tasks.prompt: Enter Interface Configuration mode for Port-channel 1.
tasks.explanation: Port-channel 1 is the logical interface representing the bundled physical links. Layer 3 or trunk settings go here.

tasks.prompt: Configure Port-channel 1 as a trunk port.
tasks.explanation: Trunk configuration on the Port-channel applies to all member links, ensuring consistent VLAN transport across the bundle.

tasks.prompt: Display a summary of all EtherChannel groups and their member ports.
tasks.explanation: 'show etherchannel summary' shows group number, protocol (LACP/PAgP), port-channel status, and member interface states.
This command verifies the logical interface is up and shows statistics aggregated across all bundled physical links.
